member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkRunnerVisibilityExtensions.RecognizedBehaviourNames
member.summary: Types that fusion.runtime isn't aware of, which need to be found using names instead.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkRunnerVisibilityExtensions.AddVisibilityNodes(Fusion.NetworkRunner,UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary: Find all component types that contribute to a scene rendering, and associate them with a component, and add them to the runner's list of visibility nodes.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkRunnerVisibilityExtensions.RefreshRunnerVisibility(Fusion.NetworkRunner,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Reapplies a runner's IsVisibile setting to all of its registered visibility nodes.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkRunnerVisibilityExtensions.CommonObjectLookup
member.summary: Dictionary lookup for manually added visibility nodes (which indicates only one instance should be visible at a time), which returns a list of nodes for a given LocalIdentifierInFile.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceAddressable`1
member.summary: An Addressables-based implementation of the asset source pattern. The asset is loaded from the Addressables system.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceAddressable`1.RuntimeKey
member.summary: Addressables runtime key. Can be used in any form Addressables supports, such as asset name, label, or address.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceAssetBundle`1
member.summary: An AssetBundle-based implementation of the asset source pattern. The asset is loaded from AssetBundles.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceAssetBundle`1.AssetBundleName
member.summary: Name of the asset bundle.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceAssetBundle`1.AssetName
member.summary: Name of the asset in the bundle.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceAssetBundle`1.NestedAssetName
member.summary: Nested asset name, for sub assets.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceAssetBundle`1.Description
member.summary: The description of the asset source. Used for debugging.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceAssetBundle`1.EditorInstance
member.summary: Returns the asset instance for Editor purposes. Does not call .

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceAssetBundle
member.summary: Utility for .

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceAssetBundle.AssetBundleRequested
member.summary: Global callback that allows for custom AssetBundle loading.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceAssetBundle.AssetUnloaded
member.summary: Global callback invoked when an asset is being unloaded.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceResource`1
member.summary: Resources-based implementation of the asset source pattern.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceResource`1.ResourcePath
member.summary: Resource path. Note that this is a Unity resource path, not a file path.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceResource`1.SubObjectName
member.summary: Sub-object name. If empty, the main object is loaded.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceResource`1.Acquire(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Loads the asset. In synchronous mode, the asset is loaded immediately. In asynchronous mode, the asset is loaded in the background.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceResource`1.Release
member.summary: Unloads the asset. If the asset is not loaded, an exception is thrown. If the asset is loaded multiple times, it is only unloaded when the last acquire is released.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceResource`1.IsCompleted
member.summary: Returns if the asset is loaded.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceResource`1.WaitForResult
member.summary: Blocks until the asset is loaded. If the asset is not loaded, an exception is thrown.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceResource`1.Description
member.summary: The description of the asset source. Used for debugging.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceResource`1.EditorInstance
member.summary: Returns the asset instance for Editor purposes. Does not call .

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceStatic`1
member.summary: Hard reference-based implementation of the asset source pattern. This asset source forms a hard reference to the asset and never releases it. This type is meant to be used at runtime. For edit-time, prefer , as it delays actually loading the asset, improving the editor performance.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceStatic`1.Object
member.summary: The asset reference. Can point to an asset or to a runtime-created object.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceStatic`1.IsCompleted
member.summary: Returns .

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceStatic`1.Acquire(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Does nothing, the asset is always loaded.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceStatic`1.Release
member.summary: Does nothing, the asset is always loaded.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceStatic`1.WaitForResult
member.summary: Returns or throws an exception if the reference is missing.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceStatic`1.EditorInstance
member.summary: Returns .

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceStaticLazy`1
member.summary: An edit-time optimised version of , taking advantage of Unity's lazy loading of assets. At runtime, this type behaves exactly like , except for the inability to use runtime-created objects.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkAssetSourceStaticLazy`1.Object
member.summary: The asset reference. Can only point to an asset, runtime-created objects will not work.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectAddressAttribute
member.summary: If applied at the assembly level, allows to be loaded with Addressables.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectAddressAttribute.#ctor(System.Type,System.String)
member.param: The type this attribute will attempt to load.
The address to load from.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectAddressAttribute.Address
member.summary: The address to load from.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectAddressAttribute.Load(System.Type)
member.summary: Loads the asset from the . Uses WaitForCompletion internally, so platforms that do not support it need to preload the address prior to loading.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectAddressAttribute.LoadAsync(System.Type)
member.summary: Loads the asset from the .

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectResourceAttribute
member.summary: If applied at the assembly level, allows to be loaded with Resources. There is a default registration for this attribute, which attempts to load the asset from Resources using path from .

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectResourceAttribute.#ctor(System.Type,System.String)
member.param: The type this attribute will attempt to load.
Resources path or /empty if path from is to be used.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectResourceAttribute.ResourcePath
member.summary: Path in Resources.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectResourceAttribute.InstantiateIfLoadedInEditor
member.summary: If loaded in the editor, should the result be instantiated instead of returning the asset itself? The default is .

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectResourceAttribute.Load(System.Type)
member.summary: Loads the asset from Resources synchronously.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectResourceAttribute.LoadAsync(System.Type)
member.summary: Loads the asset from Resources asynchronously.

member.name: M:Fusion.Statistics.FusionStatisticsPage.UpdatePage
member.summary: Centralized update for the page.

member.name: M:Fusion.Statistics.FusionStatisticsPage.RefreshView
member.summary: Refresh the page visuals. Called in the defined .

member.name: M:Fusion.Statistics.FusionStatisticsPage.AfterFusionUpdate
member.summary: Hook for callback from Fusion, used to store the statistics data to display when updating the page visual.

member.name: T:Fusion.Statistics.FusionStatistics
member.summary: Add this class to a prefab and when a game is started a statistics canvas will be initialized to display internal stats. Stats are only collected when using DEBUG dll.

member.name: T:Fusion.Statistics.StatisticsExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods to handle statistics.

member.name: M:Fusion.Statistics.StatisticsExtensions.SetupStatistics(Fusion.NetworkRunner,Fusion.Statistics.FusionStatisticsConfig)
member.summary: Set up a and create a to display the runner statistics.

member.name: M:Fusion.Statistics.StatisticsExtensions.RemoveStatistics(Fusion.NetworkRunner)
member.summary: Destroy and clean any and for this

member.name: M:Fusion.Statistics.FusionStatsLookup.GetValueText(System.Single,System.String[][],System.String)
member.summary: Convert from float number to one of the cached strings from the table.

member.name: P:Fusion.Statistics.LineChart.Title
member.summary: Chart title.

member.name: M:Fusion.Statistics.LineChart.DispatchValuesToShader
member.summary: Fills the data dispatch array with the values in the order they were added, calculate min and max and sets on the shader.

member.name: P:Fusion.Statistics.RadialChart.Title
member.summary: Chart title.

member.name: M:Fusion.Statistics.RadialChart.SetShaderValues(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Clamps between 0 and 1 based on . Considers 0 as the base value.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionBasicBillboard
member.summary: Component which automatically faces this GameObject toward the supplied Camera. If Camera == null, will face towards Camera.main.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBasicBillboard.Camera
member.summary: Force a particular camera to billboard this object toward. Leave null to use Camera.main.

member.name: T:Fusion.EnableOnSingleRunner
member.summary: Automatically adds a for each indicated component. These indicated components will be limited to no more than one enabled instance when running in Multi-Peer mode.

member.name: F:Fusion.EnableOnSingleRunner.PreferredRunner
member.summary: If more than one runner instance is visible, this indicates which peer's clone of this entity should be visible.

member.name: F:Fusion.EnableOnSingleRunner.Components
member.summary: Collection of components that will be marked for Multi-Peer mode as objects that should only have one enabled instance.

member.name: F:Fusion.EnableOnSingleRunner._guid
member.summary: Prefix for the GUIDs of components which are added at runtime.

member.name: M:Fusion.EnableOnSingleRunner.AddNodes(System.Collections.Generic.List{Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLink})
member.summary: At runtime startup, this adds a for each component reference to this GameObject.

member.name: M:Fusion.EnableOnSingleRunner.FindRecognizedTypes
member.summary: Finds visual/audio components on this GameObject, and adds them to the Components collection.

member.name: M:Fusion.EnableOnSingleRunner.FindNestedRecognizedTypes
member.summary: Finds visual/audio nested components on this GameObject and its children, and adds them to the Components collection.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionLogInitializer
member.summary: Initializes the logging system for Fusion. Use to completely override the log level and trace channels or to provide a custom logger. Use to override default Unity logger settings.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionLogInitializer.Initialize
member.summary: Initializes the logging system for Fusion. This method is called automatically when the assembly is loaded.

member.name: T:Fusion.RunnerEnableVisibility
member.summary: When running in Multi-Peer mode, this component automatically will register the associated with , and will automatically attach loaded scene objects and spawned objects with the peers visibility handling.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObjectBaker.PostprocessBehaviour(Fusion.SimulationBehaviour)
member.summary: Postprocesses the behaviour. Returns true if the object was marked dirty.

member.name: T:Fusion.RunnerAOIGizmos
member.summary: Add this Component to the NetworkRunner Prefab or GameObject. If Interest Management is enabled in NetworkProjectConfig ReplicationFeatures, gizmos will be shown that indicate active Area Of Interest cells. These gizmos are currently NOT applicable to Shared Mode and will only render for the Server/Host peer.

member.name: T:Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLinksRoot
member.summary: Flag component which indicates a NetworkObject has already been factored into a Runner's VisibilityNode list.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionRuntimeCheck
member.summary: Used to initialize Runtime flags for Fusion

member.name: T:Fusion.JsonUtilityExtensions
member.summary: Extends capabilities of by adding type annotations to the serialized JSON, Unity object reference handling and integer enquotement.

member.name: M:Fusion.JsonUtilityExtensions.EnquoteIntegers(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Enquotes integers in the JSON string that are at least long. This is useful for parsers that interpret large integers as floating point numbers.
member.param: JSON to process
Digit threshold to perfom the enquoting

member.name: M:Fusion.JsonUtilityExtensions.ToJsonWithTypeAnnotation(System.Object,Fusion.JsonUtilityExtensions.InstanceIDHandlerDelegate)
member.summary: Converts the object to JSON with type annotations.
member.param: Object to be serialized.
Handler for UnityEngine.Object references. If the handler returns an empty string, the reference is removed from the final result.

member.name: M:Fusion.JsonUtilityExtensions.ToJsonWithTypeAnnotation(System.Object,System.IO.TextWriter,System.Nullable{System.Int32},Fusion.JsonUtilityExtensions.TypeSerializerDelegate,Fusion.JsonUtilityExtensions.InstanceIDHandlerDelegate)
member.summary: Converts the object/IList to JSON with type annotations.
member.param: Object to be serialized.
The output TextWriter.
Handler for obtaining serialized type names. If , the short assembly qualified name (namespace + name + assembly name) will be used.
Handler for UnityEngine.Object references. If the handler returns an empty string, the reference is removed from the final result.

member.name: M:Fusion.JsonUtilityExtensions.FromJsonWithTypeAnnotation``1(System.String,Fusion.JsonUtilityExtensions.TypeResolverDelegate)
member.summary: Converts JSON with type annotation to an instance of . If the JSON contains type annotations, they need to match the expected result type. If there are no type annotations, use to return the expected type.
member.param: JSON to be parsed
Converts type name to a type instance.

member.name: M:Fusion.JsonUtilityExtensions.FromJsonWithTypeAnnotation(System.String,Fusion.JsonUtilityExtensions.TypeResolverDelegate)
member.summary: Converts JSON with type annotation. If there are no type annotations, use to return the expected type.
member.param: JSON to be parsed
Converts type name to a type instance.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionAddressablesUtils
member.summary: Utility class for addressables.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionAddressablesUtils.TryParseAddress(System.String,System.String@,System.String@)
member.summary: Tries to parse the address into main part and sub object name.
member.param: The address to parse.
The main part of the address.
The sub object name.
member.returns: if the address is successfully parsed; otherwise, .

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionAddressablesUtils.CreateAssetReference(System.String)
member.summary: Creates an asset reference from the given address.
member.param: The address to create the asset reference from.
member.exception: Thrown when the main part of the address is not a guid or the address is not valid.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionAddressablesUtils.LoadEditorInstance(System.String)
member.summary: Loads the editor instance for the given runtime key.
member.param: The runtime key.
member.exception: Thrown when the load editor instance handler is not set.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionAddressablesUtils.SetLoadEditorInstanceHandler(System.Func{System.String,UnityEngine.Object})
member.summary: Sets the load editor instance handler.
member.param: The load editor instance handler.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionMppmStatus
member.summary: The current status of MPPM. If the package is not enabled, this will always be .

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionMppmStatus.Disabled
member.summary: MPPM is not installed.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionMppmStatus.MainInstance
member.summary: This instance is the main instance. Can use to send commands.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionMppmStatus.VirtualInstance
member.summary: This instance is a virtual instance. Will receive commands from the main instance.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionMppm
member.summary: Support for Multiplayer Play Mode (MPPM). It uses named pipes to communicate between the main Unity instance and virtual instances.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionMppm.Status
member.summary: The current status of MPPM.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionMppm.MainEditor
member.summary: If is , this static field can be used to send commands.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionMppm.Send``1(``0)
member.summary: Sends a command to all virtual instances. Use as: FusionMppm.MainEditor?.Send

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionMppm.Broadcast``1(``0)
member.summary: Broadcasts a command to all virtual instances.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionMppmCommand
member.summary: The base class for all Fusion MPPM commands.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionMppmCommand.Execute
member.summary: Execute the command on a virtual instance. Executes synchronously.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionMppmCommand.ExecuteAsync
member.summary: Execute the command on a virtual instance. By default, calls

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionMppmCommand.NeedsAck
member.summary: Does the main instance need to wait for an ack?

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionMppmCommand.PersistentKey
member.summary: If the command is persistent (i.e. needs to be executed on each domain reload), this key is used to store it.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionMppmRegisterCustomDependencyCommand
member.summary: A command implementing a workaround for MPPM not syncing custom dependencies.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionMppmRegisterCustomDependencyCommand.DependencyName
member.summary: Name of the custom dependency.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionMppmRegisterCustomDependencyCommand.Hash
member.summary: Hash of the custom dependency.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionMppmRegisterCustomDependencyCommand.Execute
member.summary: Registers a custom dependency with the given name and hash.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionUnityEditorPaths
member.summary: Fusion Unity paths.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionUnityEditorPaths.Root
member.summary: Root folder of Fusion installation.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionUnityExtensions
member.summary: Provides backwards compatibility for Unity API.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionUnityHostProfiler
member.summary: Profiler implementation for Unity.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils
member.summary: Exension and utility methods for and types.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.SceneEqualityComparer
member.summary: A comparer that can be used when scenes need to be comparable, like when being as keys in a dictionary.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.SceneEqualityComparer.Equals(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene,UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene)
member.summary: Compares scenes by their

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.SceneEqualityComparer.GetHashCode(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene)
member.summary: Returns

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.IsAddedToBuildSettings(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene)
member.summary: Returns true if is in the build settings. Note that just checking is not enough.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.AddToBuildSettings(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene)
member.summary: Editor-only. Adds scene to the build settings.
member.returns: False if the scene is already added

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.CanBeUnloaded(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene)
member.summary: Returns true if can be unloaded.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.Dump(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene)
member.summary: Converts into string, dumping all its fields and properties.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.Dump(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.LoadSceneParameters)
member.summary: Converts to string.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.GetSceneBuildIndex(System.String)
member.summary: Gets scene's build index based on its name or path.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.GetComponents``1(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Finds all components of type in the scene.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.GetComponents``1(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene,System.Boolean,UnityEngine.GameObject[]@)
member.summary: Finds all components of type in the scene.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.GetComponents``1(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene,System.Collections.Generic.List{``0},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Finds all components of type in the scene.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionUnitySceneManagerUtils.FindComponent``1(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Finds the first instance of type in the scene. Returns null if no instance found.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionBootstrapDebugGUI
member.summary: Companion component for . Automatically added as needed for rendering in-game networking IMGUI.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrapDebugGUI.EnableHotkeys
member.summary: When enabled, the in-game user interface buttons can be activated with the keys H (Host), S (Server) and C (Client).

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrapDebugGUI.BaseSkin
member.summary: The GUISkin to use as the base for the scalable in-game UI.

member.name: T:Fusion.Matchmaking.MatchmakingArgumentsExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods to

member.name: M:Fusion.Matchmaking.MatchmakingArgumentsExtensions.SetupForFusion(Photon.Realtime.MatchmakingArguments)
member.summary: Set up a for Fusion

member.name: M:Fusion.Matchmaking.MatchmakingArgumentsExtensions.BuildRealtimeClient(Fusion.Photon.Realtime.FusionAppSettings)
member.summary: Build and Setup a Realtime Client to be used with Fusion

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault
member.summary: The default implementation of . Support single and multiple peer mode. For the latter, each runner is allocated a meta scene with isolated physics.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.IsSceneTakeOverEnabled
member.summary: If enabled and there is an already loaded scene that matches what the scene manager has intended to load, that scene will be used instead and load will be avoided.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.LogSceneLoadErrors
member.summary: Should all scene load errors be logged into the console? If disabled, errors can still be retrieved via the or .

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault._allOwnedScenes
member.summary: All the scenes loaded by all the managers. Used when is enabled.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault._multiPeerSceneRoots
member.summary: In multiple peer mode, each runner maintains its own scene where all the newly loaded scenes are moved to. This is to make sure physics are properly sandboxed.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault._runningCoroutines
member.summary: List of running coroutines. Only one is actually executed at a time.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault._tempUnloadScene
member.summary: For remote clients, this manager first unloads old scenes then loads the new ones. It might happen that all the current scenes need to be unloaded and in such case a temp scene needs to be created to ensure at least one scene loaded at all times.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.MultiPeerScene
member.summary: Scene used when Multiple Peer mode is used. Each loaded scene is merged into this one, allowing for multiple runners to have separate cross-scene physics.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.MultiPeerDontDestroyOnLoadRoot
member.summary: Root for DontDestroyOnLoad objects. Instantiated on .

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.Runner
member.summary: The runner this manager is assigned to.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.Initialize(Fusion.NetworkRunner)
member.summary: Assigns the runner and, in multiple peer mode, creates a new meta-scene to isolate physics.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.Shutdown
member.summary: Closes all the scenes previously loaded by the manager. If there are not any more loaded scenes, it creates a temporary "FusionSceneManager_TempEmptyScene" scene.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.IsBusy
member.summary: Returns true if a scene is being loaded or, in multiple peer mode, if there are no meta-scenes to spawn on.

member.name: P:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.MainRunnerScene
member.summary: In multiple peer mode returns a meta scene associated with the runner, otherwise returns

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.IsRunnerScene(UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene)
member.summary: In multiple peer mode returns true if matches . In single peer mode, always returns true.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.TryGetPhysicsScene2D(UnityEngine.PhysicsScene2D@)
member.summary: Sets to physics scene.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.TryGetPhysicsScene3D(UnityEngine.PhysicsScene@)
member.summary: Sets to physics scene.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.MakeDontDestroyOnLoad(UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary: In multiple peer, moves the object to a root that is not associated with any scenes. In single peer, simply calls

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.MoveGameObjectToScene(UnityEngine.GameObject,Fusion.SceneRef)
member.summary: Finds a scene matching and moves there. In multiple peer the object will be also assigned a meta-scene root parent object.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.LoadScene(Fusion.SceneRef,Fusion.NetworkLoadSceneParameters)
member.summary: Wrapper around

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.UnloadScene(Fusion.SceneRef)
member.summary: Wrapper around

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.GetSceneRef(System.String)
member.summary: Converts name/path to a . If Addressables are enabled and the scene is not in the build settings, addressable scenes with label will be discovered and then taken under consideration. Since this might result in a synchronous wait, consider awaiting before running the simulation.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.GetSceneRef(UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary: For single peer mode redirects to . For multiple peer performs a direct check on each of meta-scene root objects.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.OnSceneInfoChanged(Fusion.NetworkSceneInfo,Fusion.NetworkSceneInfoChangeSource)
member.summary: Does nothing. Implement this method and return true if you want to handle scene info changes manually. See also .

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.LoadSceneCoroutine(Fusion.SceneRef,Fusion.NetworkLoadSceneParameters)
member.summary: The actual scene loading takes place here. Simulates unloading scenes for in multiple peer mode, checks if any of already loaded scenes can be taken over (). For addressable scenes, it will attempt to load them with Addressables API. Once done, invokes , which can be used for scene post-processing. Progress can be handled with .

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.UnloadSceneCoroutine(Fusion.SceneRef)
member.summary: Finds the actual scene to unload. If this is the only scene loaded, a temporary empty scene will be created.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.OnSceneLoaded(Fusion.SceneRef,UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene,Fusion.NetworkLoadSceneParameters)
member.summary: Post-processes the loaded scene. Overrides need to call the base implementation.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.OnLoadSceneProgress(Fusion.SceneRef,System.Single)
member.summary: Implement to handle the scene loading process

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.MakeLoadingScope
member.summary: Creates a scope in which the scene manager will report itself as .

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.MarkSceneAsOwned(Fusion.SceneRef,UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene)
member.summary: A scene needs to be "owned" in order for the scene manager to properly track it.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.AddressableScenesLabel
member.summary: A label by which addressable scenes can be discovered.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.LoadAddressableScenePathsAsync
member.summary: Starts looking for addressable scenes marked with . Since this is an asynchronous operation, it is advised to call this method before attempting to load any addressable scenes. Otherwise, the caller might have to perform a synchronous wait, which may not be supported on some platforms (WebGL).

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.GetAddressableScenes
member.returns: A task representing resolve operation and optionally a delegate to be invoked before the task is going to be awaited synchronously

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.GetAddressableScenePathsTimeout
member.summary: Returns the timeout for addressable scene paths to be resolved. By default, this method returns 10 seconds.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.GetAddressableScenesResult.Task
member.summary: A task to be awaited when the scene paths are needed.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.GetAddressableScenesResult.BeforeWaitForCompletion
member.summary: In some cases getting a result from a task returned by Addressables will result in a deadlock. What helps is explicitly calling WaitForCompletion beforehand and this is what does.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.MultiPeerSceneRoot
member.summary: A scene root script. In multiple peer mode, each scene is placed on a meta-scene, which a root object per each game scene.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.MultiPeerSceneRoot.SceneRef
member.summary: The associated scene ref.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.MultiPeerSceneRoot.ScenePath
member.summary: The scene path used to load the scene.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.MultiPeerSceneRoot.SceneHandle
member.summary: The scene handle.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkSceneManagerDefault.MultiPeerSceneRoot.Scene
member.summary: The scene object. When a scene is unloaded becomes invalid.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionBootstrap
member.summary: A Fusion prototyping class for starting up basic networking. Add this component to your startup scene, and supply a . Can be set to automatically startup the network, display an in-game menu, or allow simplified start calls like .

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.StartModes
member.summary: Selection for how will behave at startup.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.Stage
member.summary: The current stage of connection or shutdown.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.RunnerPrefab
member.summary: Supply a Prefab or a scene object which has the component on it, as well as any runner dependent components which implement , such as or your own custom INetworkInput implementations.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.StartMode
member.summary: Select how network startup will be triggered. Automatically, by in-game menu selection, or exclusively by script.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.AutoStartAs
member.summary: When is set to , this option selects if the will be started as a dedicated server, or as a host (which is a server with a local player).

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.AutoHideGUI
member.summary: will not render GUI elements while == .

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.AutoClients
member.summary: The number of client instances that will be created if running in Multi-Peer Mode. When using the Select start mode, this number will be the default value for the additional clients option box.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.ClientStartDelay
member.summary: How long to wait after starting a peer before starting the next one.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.ServerPort
member.summary: The port that server/host will use.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.DefaultRoomName
member.summary: The default room name to use when connecting to photon cloud.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.InitialScenePath
member.summary: The Scene that will be loaded after network shutdown completes (all peers have disconnected). If this field is null or invalid, will be set to the current scene when runs Awake().

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrap._currentStage
member.summary: Indicates which step of the startup process is currently in.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.AutoConnectVirtualInstances
member.summary: Requires Multiplayer Play Mode (MPPM) to be installed. If enabled, will automatically join the virtual instance.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.VirtualInstanceConnectDelay
member.summary: How much to wait before the main instance lets the virtual instances connect.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.CurrentStage
member.summary: Indicates which step of the startup process is currently in.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.LastCreatedClientIndex
member.summary: The index number used for the last created peer.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.CurrentServerMode
member.summary: The server mode that was used for initial startup. Used to inform UI which client modes should be available.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.StartSinglePlayer
member.summary: Start a single player instance.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.StartServer
member.summary: Start a server instance.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.StartHost
member.summary: Start a host instance. This is a server instance, with a local player.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.StartClient
member.summary: Start a client instance.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.StartServerPlusClients
member.summary: Start a Fusion server instance, and the number of client instances indicated by . InstanceMode must be set to Multi-Peer mode, as this requires multiple instances.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.StartHostPlusClients
member.summary: Start a Fusion host instance, and the number of client instances indicated by . InstanceMode must be set to Multi-Peer mode, as this requires multiple instances.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.StartServerPlusClients(System.Int32)
member.summary: Start a Fusion server instance, and the indicated number of client instances. InstanceMode must be set to Multi-Peer mode, as this requires multiple instances.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.StartHostPlusClients(System.Int32)
member.summary: Start a Fusion host instance (server with local player), and the indicated number of additional client instances. InstanceMode must be set to Multi-Peer mode, as this requires multiple instances.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.StartMultipleClients(System.Int32)
member.summary: Start a Fusion host instance (server with local player), and the indicated number of additional client instances. InstanceMode must be set to Multi-Peer mode, as this requires multiple instances.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.StartMultipleSharedClients(System.Int32)
member.summary: Start as Room on the Photon cloud, and connects as one or more clients.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionBootstrap.ShouldShowGUI
member.summary: Only show the GUI if the StartMode is set to UserInterface and not being run in a Virtual Instance (MPPM).

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkPrefabSourceStatic
member.summary: Hard reference-based implementation of the asset source pattern. This asset source forms a hard reference to the asset and never releases it. This type is meant to be used at runtime. For edit-time, prefer , as it delays actually loading the asset, improving the editor performance.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkPrefabSourceStaticLazy
member.summary: An edit-time optimised version of , taking advantage of Unity's lazy loading of assets. At runtime, this type behaves exactly like , except for the inability to use runtime-created objects.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkPrefabSourceResource
member.summary: Resources-based implementation of the asset source pattern.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkPrefabSourceAssetBundle
member.summary: An AssetBundle implementation of the asset source pattern.

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkPrefabSourceAddressable
member.summary: An Addressables-based implementation of the asset source pattern. The asset is loaded from the Addressables system.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionScalableIMGUI
member.summary: In-Game IMGUI style used for the interface.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionScalableIMGUI.GetScaledSkin(UnityEngine.GUISkin,System.Single@,System.Single@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Single@)
member.summary: Get the custom scalable skin, already resized to the current screen. Provides the height, width, padding and margin used.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionScalableIMGUI.ScaleGuiSkinToScreenHeight
member.summary: Modifies a skin to make it scale with screen height.
member.returns: Returns (height, width, padding, top-margin, left-box-margin) values applied to the GuiSkin

member.name: T:Fusion.NetworkObjectProviderDefault
member.summary: The default implementation of . Uses to acquire prefab instances. Pooling is not implemented by default, but can be implemented in virtual method.

member.name: F:Fusion.NetworkObjectProviderDefault.DelayIfSceneManagerIsBusy
member.summary: If enabled, the provider will delay acquiring a prefab instance if the scene manager is busy.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObjectProviderDefault.AcquireInstance(Fusion.NetworkRunner,Fusion.NetworkObjectAcquireContext@,Fusion.NetworkObject@)
member.summary: If points to a scene object, returns . Otherwise, uses to go acquire a prefab and then calls .

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObjectProviderDefault.CreatePrefabInstance(Fusion.NetworkRunner,Fusion.NetworkPrefabId,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Uses to load a prefab instance and then calls to create an instance.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObjectProviderDefault.ReleaseInstance(Fusion.NetworkRunner,Fusion.NetworkObjectReleaseContext@)
member.summary: If is false, calls one of Destroy methods, depending what type of object is being dealt with. To implement pooling, it may be better to override Destroy methods instead.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObjectProviderDefault.GetPrefabId(Fusion.NetworkRunner,Fusion.NetworkObjectGuid)
member.summary: Uses to translate to .

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObjectProviderDefault.InstantiatePrefab(Fusion.NetworkRunner,Fusion.NetworkObject)
member.summary: Calls . Override to alter this behaviour.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObjectProviderDefault.DestroyPrefabInstance(Fusion.NetworkRunner,Fusion.NetworkPrefabId,Fusion.NetworkObject)
member.summary: Calls . Override to alter this behaviour.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObjectProviderDefault.DestroyPrefabNestedObject(Fusion.NetworkRunner,Fusion.NetworkPrefabId,System.Int32,Fusion.NetworkObject)
member.summary: Calls . Override to alter this behaviour.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObjectProviderDefault.DestroyPrefabNestedObject(Fusion.NetworkRunner,Fusion.NetworkObject)
member.summary: Calls . Override to alter this behaviour.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObjectProviderDefault.DestroySceneObject(Fusion.NetworkRunner,Fusion.NetworkSceneObjectId,Fusion.NetworkObject)
member.summary: Calls . Override to alter this behaviour.

member.name: M:Fusion.NetworkObjectProviderDefault.AcquirePrefabInstance(Fusion.NetworkRunner,Fusion.NetworkPrefabAcquireContext@,Fusion.NetworkObject@)
member.summary: Legacy API implementation.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionUnityLogger
member.summary: Fusion logger implementation for Unity.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionUnityLogger.LogActiveRunnerTick
member.summary: If true, the active runner's tick will be logged.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionUnityLogger.LogActiveRunnerLocalPlayer
member.summary: If true, the active runner's local player will be logged.

member.name: T:Fusion.IRunnerVisibilityRecognizedType
member.summary: Flags a MonoBehaviour class as a RunnerVisibilityControl recognized type. Will be included in runner visibility handling, and will be found by component finds.

member.name: T:Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLink
member.summary: Identifies visible/audible components (such as renderers, canvases, lights) that should be enabled/disabled by runner visibility handling. Automatically added to scene objects and spawned objects during play if running in . Additionally this component can be added manually at development time to identify specific Behaviours or Renderers you would like to restrict to one enabled copy at a time.

member.name: T:Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLink.PreferredRunners
member.summary: The peer runner that will be used if more than one runner is visible, and this node was manually added by developer (indicating only one instance should be visible at a time).

member.name: F:Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLink.PreferredRunners.Auto
member.summary: The first visible runner will be used.

member.name: F:Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLink.PreferredRunners.Server
member.summary: The server peer/runner will be used if visible.

member.name: F:Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLink.PreferredRunners.Client
member.summary: The first client peer/runner will be used if visible.

member.name: F:Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLink.PreferredRunners.InputAuthority
member.summary: The components will only be enabled on the instance that has input authority over the NetworkObject. Unlike the other options, this expects a NetworkObject to work and it will search its children and parents for it.

member.name: F:Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLink.PreferredRunner
member.summary: If more than one runner instance is visible, this indicates which peer's clone of this entity should be visible.

member.name: F:Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLink.Component
member.summary: The associated component with this node. This Behaviour or Renderer will be enabled/disabled when its NetworkRunner.IsVisible value is changed.

member.name: F:Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLink.Guid
member.summary: Guid is used for common objects (user flagged components that should only run in one instance), to identify matching clones.

member.name: P:Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLink.DefaultState
member.summary: Set to false to indicate that this object should remain disabled even when a runner is visible.

member.name: M:Fusion.RunnerVisibilityLink.SetEnabled(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets the visibility state of this node.

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionMenuSceneInfo
member.summary: Info asset for creating configurable selectable scenes in the Photon menu. This type was moved to the SDK to simplify adding scenes automatically (e.g. when installing another unity package).

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionMenuSceneInfo.Name
member.summary: Displayed scene name.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionMenuSceneInfo.NameOrSceneName
member.summary: Returns either or .

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionMenuSceneInfo.ScenePath
member.summary: The path to the scene asset.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionMenuSceneInfo.SceneName
member.summary: Gets the filename of the ScenePath to set as Unity scene to load during connection sequence.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionMenuSceneInfo.Preview
member.summary: The sprite displayed as scene preview in the scene selection UI.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionMenuSceneInfo.IsHidden
member.summary: Don't use this scene.
